_provenance.note: The study's per-role provider/model bind record, sanitized for the public tree. stream_role_binds is the authoritative role->model map for the published run, recovered from the per-configuration scorecards in the Zenodo deposit (data/consolidated_armT.json / _armR.json, identical binds in both arms). reference_workspace_binds is the pre-study bind readout of the reference workspace the streams were cloned from. The published report does not name the commercial scalar-reward baseline vendor pending vendor notice ('scalar-reward' is the placeholder label, matching the published record); this repo's provider plane is generic product integration. Apply binds via POST /v1/roles/bind — see REPRODUCING.md.
_provenance.source: Zenodo DOI 10.5281/zenodo.21270268 (data/consolidated_arm{T,R}.json) + the rescued pre-study bind readout
_provenance.sanitization: the scalar-reward baseline vendor is not named (pending vendor notice); no other changes

physician_arm_note: The ten physician-curated cases (withheld pending consent; see REPRODUCING.md) were graded with the dated deployment gpt-5.5-2026-04-23.

notes.workspace_naming: The setup script (repro/setup_streams.py) authors the published workspace and role names directly ('stream-a-single'..'baseline-scalar-reward'; 'single_generalist', the six 'ens_*' members, 'cs_*'/'cm_*', 'scalar_reward_baseline'). stream_role_binds above is the authoritative role-to-model map.
notes.provider_labels: azure = Azure OpenAI deployment; openai_compatible = an OpenAI-compatible endpoint (the study served Llama-4-Maverick and Mistral-Large-3 through one); the reference workspace recorded its OpenAI-compatible binds under the label 'openai'.
